blocks.title: Definition
blocks.content: Ratio is a way of comparing two or more quantities of the same kind. It tells us how many times one quantity is as compared to the other.

blocks.title: Notation
blocks.content: Ratio is denoted by the symbol ':' (colon). Example: The ratio of 10 to 20 is written as 10:20, which is read as 10 is to 20.

blocks.title: Representation
blocks.description: A ratio can also be expressed as a fraction.

blocks.title: Example 1
steps: Divide both terms by their HCF (10)

blocks.title: Quick Revision
points: Ratio compares two quantities of the same kind.
It is expressed in simplest form by dividing by the HCF.
The order of terms in a ratio is important.

blocks.title: Rule
blocks.content: A ratio is simplified by dividing both terms by their Highest Common Factor (HCF).

blocks.title: Example 2
steps: Multiply both terms by 10 to remove decimal: 25:35
blocks.formula_used: Remove decimals first, then divide by HCF

blocks.title: Key Points
points: Always simplify ratios to their lowest terms.
For decimals, multiply by a power of 10 first.
The simplified ratio is equivalent to the original ratio.

blocks.title: Definition
blocks.content: A proportion is an equation that states that two ratios are equal.

blocks.title: Notation
blocks.content: If a:b = c:d, then it is written as a:b :: c:d. It is read as 'a is to b as c is to d'.

blocks.title: Terms of a Proportion
blocks.description: Here, a and d are the extremes, and b and c are the means.

blocks.title: Example 1
blocks.find: Check if it is a proportion.
steps: Since the products are equal, it is a proportion.
blocks.formula_used: Product of Extremes = Product of Means

blocks.title: Remember
points: In a proportion, the product of means equals the product of extremes.
This is the key property used to solve many problems.
If a:b :: c:d, then ad = bc.

blocks.title: Mean Proportional Formula
blocks.description: The mean proportional between a and b is the number m such that a:m :: m:b.

blocks.title: Key Point
points: The mean proportional of a and b is the geometric mean.
It is the square root of the product of the two numbers.

blocks.title: Third Proportional Formula
blocks.formula: If a:b :: b:c, then c = b² / a
blocks.description: c is the third proportional to a and b.

blocks.title: Remember
points: Third proportional is the fourth term in a proportion when the first two terms are a and b.
Formula: c = b²/a.

blocks.title: Direct Proportion
blocks.subtitle: As One Increases, So Does the Other

blocks.title: Concept
blocks.content: Two quantities are in direct proportion if an increase in one causes a proportional increase in the other, or a decrease in one causes a proportional decrease in the other.

blocks.title: Direct Proportion Formula
blocks.formula: If x ∝ y, then x = ky or x / y = k (constant)
blocks.description: The ratio of the two quantities remains constant.

blocks.title: Example 1
given: If 5 pens cost ₹50, what is the cost of 8 pens?
steps: More pens means more cost. It is a direct proportion.
Let cost of 8 pens be x.

blocks.title: Example 2
given: A car covers 120 km in 2 hours. How far will it go in 5 hours?
steps: More time means more distance. Direct proportion.

blocks.title: Key Points
points: In direct proportion, the ratio of the quantities is constant.
If one quantity doubles, the other also doubles.
Graph is a straight line passing through the origin.

blocks.title: Inverse Proportion
blocks.subtitle: As One Increases, the Other Decreases

blocks.title: Concept
blocks.content: Two quantities are in inverse proportion if an increase in one causes a proportional decrease in the other, and vice versa.

blocks.title: Inverse Proportion Formula
blocks.description: The product of the two quantities remains constant.

blocks.title: Example 1
given: 10 men can complete a work in 30 days. How many days will 15 men take?
steps: More men means less days. Inverse proportion.
Let days for 15 men be x.

blocks.title: Example 2
given: 5 pipes fill a tank in 12 hours. How much time will 8 pipes take?
steps: More pipes means less time. Inverse proportion.

blocks.title: Common Mistake
blocks.wrong: Using direct proportion for work and time problems.
blocks.correct: Always identify that more workers means less time—it's an inverse proportion.
blocks.tip: Read the problem carefully to understand the relationship.

blocks.title: Quick Revision
points: In inverse proportion, the product of the quantities is constant.
If one quantity doubles, the other halves.
Common examples: Speed and Time, Men and Days, Pipes and Time.

blocks.title: Concept
blocks.content: Compound proportion involves more than two quantities where some are directly proportional and some are inversely proportional to each other.

blocks.title: Example 1
given: 20 men can build a wall 100 m long in 10 days. How many men are needed to build a wall 200 m long in 5 days?
steps: More men for more work (direct). More men for less days (inverse).
Let the required number of men be x.

blocks.title: Example 2
given: 4 machines produce 200 units in 5 hours. How many units will 6 machines produce in 10 hours?
steps: More machines means more units (direct). More hours means more units (direct).

blocks.title: Key Points
points: Identify which quantities are directly proportional and which are inversely proportional.
Set up the equation by multiplying direct quantities and dividing inverse quantities.
Practice is key to mastering compound proportions.

blocks.title: Concept
blocks.content: In a partnership, profits and losses are shared in the ratio of investments, after adjusting for time.

blocks.title: Partnership Ratio Formula
blocks.description: If time is equal, profit is shared in the ratio of investments.

blocks.title: Example 1
given: A invests ₹20,000 and B invests ₹30,000. They share profits equally over time.
steps: Since time is equal, profit ratio = 2:3

blocks.title: Example 2
given: A invests ₹10,000 for 6 months and B invests ₹15,000 for 4 months.

blocks.title: Remember
points: Profit is shared in the ratio of capital invested and the time for which it is invested.
If only capital is given, time is assumed to be equal.

blocks.title: Concept
blocks.content: When we have ratios like a:b and b:c, we can combine them to get a:b:c.

blocks.title: Example 1
steps: Make the 'b' term equal in both ratios.

blocks.title: Example 2
steps: Make B and C equal across ratios.

blocks.title: Key Point
points: To combine ratios, make the common term equal in both ratios.
Use the LCM of the common terms to make them equal.

blocks.title: Importance
blocks.content: Ratio and Proportion is the foundation of many topics like Profit & Loss, Time & Work, Speed & Distance, Partnership, and Mixtures.

blocks.title: Exam Insight
points: Ratio and Proportion is one of the most important aptitude topics.
Many arithmetic chapters directly use ratio and proportion concepts.
Mastering this topic improves speed and accuracy in all problems.

blocks.title: Common Shortcuts
points: In a proportion, product of extremes = product of means.
For direct proportion, x₁/y₁ = x₂/y₂.
For inverse proportion, x₁y₁ = x₂y₂.
To combine ratios, make the common term equal.
In partnerships, profit is shared in the ratio of capital × time.
Mean proportional = √(a × b).
Third proportional = b² / a.

blocks.title: Speed Trick
blocks.wrong: Assuming any ratio can be added directly.
blocks.correct: Ratios must be combined by making the common term equal.
blocks.tip: Always simplify ratios to their lowest terms for easier calculations.

blocks.question: If a:b = 2:3 and b:c = 4:5, find a:c.
What is the mean proportional between 9 and 16?
If 10 men can complete a work in 20 days, in how many days will 8 men complete the same work?
blocks.explanation: Men and days are inversely proportional. 10 × 20 = 8 × x => x = 25 days.

blocks.question: If a:b = 3:4 and b:c = 6:7, find a:b:c.
A sum of ₹4000 is divided between A and B in the ratio 2:3. What is A's share?
Two numbers are in the ratio 5:7. If the sum is 120, find the larger number.
A mixture contains milk and water in the ratio 5:3. If the total quantity is 40 liters, find the quantity of water.

blocks.title: Must Remember
points: Ratio compares two quantities of the same kind.
A ratio is expressed in simplest form.
Proportion is an equality of two ratios.
In a proportion, product of extremes = product of means.
Mean proportional = √(a × b).
Third proportional = b² / a.
Direct Proportion: x₁/y₁ = x₂/y₂.
Inverse Proportion: x₁y₁ = x₂y₂.
Compound proportion handles multiple quantities.
Partnership profits are shared in the ratio of Investment × Time.
To combine ratios, make the common term equal.
Ratio and Proportion is the foundation of many arithmetic topics.

blocks.title: Most Common Exam Trap
blocks.wrong: Adding ratios directly without making terms equal.
blocks.correct: Always make the common term equal before combining ratios.
blocks.tip: Use the LCM method to combine ratios efficiently.
